articles.title: Heatwave Alert: Western Odisha Reels Under Extreme Temperatures - Kanak News Odisha
articles.full_text:
A severe heatwave has taken hold of Western Odisha, pushing temperatures to alarming levels.
Several districts are witnessing scorching conditions, with the mercury soaring well above normal. The intense heat has disrupted daily life and raised health concerns among residents.

articles.title: Returns home from census, Odisha school headmaster dies due to suspected heatstroke - Odisha TV
articles.full_text:
In a disturbing incident, a school headmaster from Subarnapur district allegedly died after returning from census duty on Sunday, with family members alleging that heatstroke caused his death.
The deceased teacher has been identified as Bansidhar Bhoi, a resident of Bamaraj village under Ullunda block. He was serving as the headmaster of Sunapali Primary School.
According to family sources, the headmaster Bhoi had gone to Sunapali village earlier in the day to carry out census-related works. After returning home in the afternoon, he began to feel unwell. As his condition deteriorated, the family members rushed him to a health facility in Biramaharajpur. However, doctors at the hospital declared him ‘dead’ while he was undergoing treatment.
The family has alleged that the man succumbed to heatstroke after working under extreme summer conditions. The region has been experiencing intense heat, raising concerns about the safety of field-level workers engaged in outdoor duties.
Headmaster Bhoi is survived by his wife and two unmarried daughters. His sudden death has left the family in distress, prompting demands for government compensation and support.
Residents and the local community have urged the authorities to take necessary precautions for personnel assigned to outdoor government duties during the peak summer months.
Police officials have not yet confirmed the exact cause of death. Further medical reports are awaited, and appropriate action is expected based on findings.
“Patient Bansidhar Bhoi came to the Emergency OPD at 5.30 PM. He was breathless upon arrival. He is a known case of Diabetes Mellitus and Hypertension. He stated that he had participated in a census survey today and also yesterday. After his admission, we provided the necessary treatment. Following the treatment, the patient's SPO2 significantly dropped all of a sudden,” Dr Saroj Kumar Panigrahi said to OTV when contacted.
“Despite the treatment we administered, the patient suddenly collapsed. He was declared dead at around 7.40 PM. We were trying to refer him, but the ‘108 Ambulance’ vehicle could not arrive on time. We had made arrangements to refer him to a higher health facility, but he could not be shifted. We have already informed the police of a post-mortem. The exact cause of death will be known tomorrow once the post-mortem report comes in,” the doctor treating the headmaster expressed.
